extra.reasoning: The property requires that flipping rash false->true never lowers acuity. rash appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop [priority 100] IF (palpitations AND dizziness AND severe_headache AND chest_pain AND vomiting AND rash) THEN acuity := self_care. This is the highest-priority rule and sets the LOWEST acuity. Witness input (the rash=true case shown): palpitations=T, dizziness=T, severe_headache=T, chest_pain=T, vomiting=T, leg_swelling=F, dyspnea=F, slurred_speech=F. With rash=FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire; matching rules are raise_chest_pain (pri 3, emergent), raise_dizziness (pri 3, emergent), plus several pri-2 urgent rules; highest priority 3 wins -> acuity = emergent. With rash=TRUE (all else identical): all six conjuncts hold, so interaction_drop (priority 100) fires and beats every priority-3 rule -> acuity = self_care. Flipping rash from false to true drops acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), which lowers acuity. Property violated.
